headline: French hospitals at 'tipping point' after seven days of heatwave
description: Emergency services saw the health consequences of the heatwave accelerate within 24 hours, between Wednesday and Thursday. The prime minister activated level 3 of the ORSAN plan, which is designed to increase hospital staffing levels.

headline: Heatwave: Macron and PM Lecornu on the defensive after criticism over France's lack of preparation
Ukraine's Donbas rear area, from Sloviansk to Kramatorsk, is under constant Russian pressure
How cities in Africa are adapting to intense and deadly heat
description: Reflective paints, green roofs, work breaks: Many African countries are seeking innovative ways to cope with increasingly extreme temperatures.

headline: US Supreme Court clears way for Trump administration's anti-immigration policies
description: In a historic reversal of the country's tradition of welcoming newcomers, on Thursday, the justices authorized the administration's move to revoke protections that shielded 350,000 Haitians and 6,000 Syrians from deportation.

headline: 250 years of American independence: Thomas Jefferson and John Adams, 'the North and South poles of the American Revolution'
description: 'America 250' (4/13). Fifty years to the day after the adoption of the Declaration of Independence, two of its architects died: the enigmatic Thomas Jefferson and the impulsive John Adams. This coincidence only strengthened, among the population, the sense of an unprecedented destiny promised to their nation.

headline: Musée d'Orsay faces demand for restitution of Van Gogh painting
description: The descendant of a German Jewish art collector persecuted by the Nazis is seeking the return of the painting 'Hôpital Saint-Paul à Saint-Rémy-de-Provence.' The Commission for the Compensation of Victims of Spoliation is expected to review the case starting in September.

'Impossible to sleep': A night in sweltering France, in search of a cool spot
description: With a heatwave bearing down, the French have taken to living after dark, chasing relief from the sun's lingering grip. From Paris to Saint-Malo, by way of Orléans, parks and pools stirred to life as soon as the day ended.

headline: France faces unprecedented heatwave: 'We are about to experience four of the hottest days ever recorded in the country'
description: A prolonged heatwave, rather than a brief spike, has arrived. Unusually high temperatures are set to persist across the country until at least the end of this week, exposing more than 90% of the French population to extreme and rare heat.

headline: Living under Paris's zinc roofs in 40°C heat: 'One day, the people living there will look for those responsible'
description: As France faces extreme heat, many of these top-story spaces have become uninhabitable. Some residents have formed groups to call on their landlords, neighbors and local authorities to address their living conditions.
